home.title: Regular expression visualizer
home.description: Visualize regular expressions as railroad diagrams — modern syntax, live preview.

api.regexToSvg: One-shot: regex → standalone SVG string; null if invalid or semantically broken.
api.parseRegex: Parse to an AST and validate; the result has ok, ast and issues (syntax + semantic).
api.lintRegex: Just the semantic issues (never-matching assertions, empty classes, …).
api.buildDiagram: Lay out a regex AST into a railroad-diagram model.
api.renderToSvg: Render a diagram model to an SVG string.
api.toRegexLiteral: Build a /…/flags literal from source + flags.

ref.title: Syntax reference
ref.subtitle: Click any token to insert it at the cursor.

items.set: one of a, b or c
items.range: one letter from a to z
items.negSet: any char except a, b, c

lint.assertionNeverMatches: This assertion can never be satisfied — the regex will never match.

explain.title: Explanation
explain.invalid: Fix the regex to see an explanation.

usage.subtitle: The diagram engine is a standalone npm package.
usage.intro: It parses a regex into an AST, lays it out as a railroad diagram, and renders a self-contained, themeable SVG — plus syntax/semantic validation and a token-by-token plain-language explanation. Framework-agnostic, usable in any JS/TS project.
usage.featureSyntax: Modern syntax: named groups, lookbehind, Unicode properties, v-flag set operations
usage.featureSvg: Self-contained SVG, themeable via CSS variables (incl. dark mode), exportable
usage.featureLint: Parsing validates too: syntax errors and semantic issues (never-matching assertions, empty classes)
usage.featureAgnostic: Framework-agnostic pure functions, zero runtime dependencies
usage.exRenderDesc: Get a self-contained SVG string in one call — drop it into the page or save it to a file.
usage.exValidateDesc: parseRegex returns syntax errors and semantic issues alike (e.g. an assertion that never matches).
usage.exExplainDesc: Get a token-by-token explanation and print it indented by depth.
usage.exLowerDesc: Two steps: lay out the AST into a model, then render it to SVG yourself.

test.title: Test strings
test.placeholder: One input per line — each line is validated for a match.
